scenario: interaction-artifact graph for AI-assisted software work

cases.name: causal ancestors along a named relation
cases.current_v1_1_expression: List[Traverse["output:design-doc-v4", "caused_by"], ForEach[Traverse["output:design-doc-v4", "caused_by"], parent_, Traverse[parent_, "caused_by"]], ForEach[ForEach[Traverse["output:design-doc-v4", "caused_by"], parent_, Traverse[parent_, "caused_by"]], grandparent_, Traverse[grandparent_, "caused_by"]]]

expected.canonical_expression: List[Traverse["output:design-doc-v4", "caused_by"], ForEach[Traverse["output:design-doc-v4", "caused_by"], parent_, Traverse[parent_, "caused_by"]], ForEach[ForEach[Traverse["output:design-doc-v4", "caused_by"], parent_, Traverse[parent_, "caused_by"]], grandparent_, Traverse[grandparent_, "caused_by"]]]

cases.pressure_note: A three-hop causal query repeats traversal boilerplate and loses the user's causal intent in generic List/ForEach nesting.
cases.promotion_risk: Named recursion depth, scope, and cycle behavior must be explicit before any operator promotion.
cases.conformance_test_sketch: Given a fixture graph with a finite caused_by chain and a cycle guard, CausalAncestors[target, relation, {max_depth:3}] should produce the same ordered ancestor set as the expanded v1.1.0 expression.

cases.name: revision chain traversal
cases.current_v1_1_expression: List[Traverse["output:adr-0004-v3", "revises"], ForEach[Traverse["output:adr-0004-v3", "revises"], previous_, Traverse[previous_, "revises"]], ForEach[ForEach[Traverse["output:adr-0004-v3", "revises"], previous_, Traverse[previous_, "revises"]], older_, Traverse[older_, "revises"]]]

expected.canonical_expression: List[Traverse["output:adr-0004-v3", "revises"], ForEach[Traverse["output:adr-0004-v3", "revises"], previous_, Traverse[previous_, "revises"]], ForEach[ForEach[Traverse["output:adr-0004-v3", "revises"], previous_, Traverse[previous_, "revises"]], older_, Traverse[older_, "revises"]]]

cases.pressure_note: Revision history is a recurring graph shape where generated expressions are verbose and easy to accidentally under-bound.
cases.promotion_risk: A shortcut must not assume a storage-specific timestamp or revision field; edge name and max depth must remain explicit.
cases.conformance_test_sketch: Given a linear v3->v2->v1 revises chain, RevisionChain should return the same chain as the expanded traversal expression under the same max_depth.

cases.name: manual provenance write for a durable output
cases.pressure_note: Every durable output write repeats the same provenance edge-laying pattern, increasing omission risk.
cases.promotion_risk: A provenance helper must not collapse host audit metadata and in-graph provenance into the same layer.
cases.conformance_test_sketch: Given a strict provenance profile, CreateWithProvenance should expand to a node plus produced/participated_in edges and preserve the same write-review preflight posture.

cases.name: strict provenance reject pressure
cases.pressure_note: Current v1.1.0 can require review for writes, but it cannot express a profile rule that rejects durable outputs missing in-graph provenance.
cases.promotion_risk: Strict provenance belongs to profile/preflight integration unless repeated evidence shows it should become a core write concern.
cases.conformance_test_sketch: Under a strict-provenance profile, a durable_output Create without produced_by provenance should reject during preflight with a deterministic provenance.missing reason.

cases.name: reviewer point-of-view projection
cases.current_v1_1_expression: List[Query[n_, Equal[Get[n_, "kind"], "decision"], {"k":25, "mode":"default"}], Query[e_, Equal[Get[e_, "relation_type"], "constrains"], {"k":25, "mode":"default"}], Query[e_, Equal[Get[e_, "relation_type"], "blocks"], {"k":25, "mode":"default"}]]

expected.canonical_expression: List[Query[n_, Equal[Get[n_, "kind"], "decision"], {"k": 25, "mode": "default"}], Query[e_, Equal[Get[e_, "relation_type"], "constrains"], {"k": 25, "mode": "default"}], Query[e_, Equal[Get[e_, "relation_type"], "blocks"], {"k": 25, "mode": "default"}]]

cases.pressure_note: Role-specific views repeat private filter bundles and cannot be checked as named capabilities.
cases.promotion_risk: POV registration should start as profile/capability integration; otherwise CogLang risks becoming a UI authorization language.
cases.conformance_test_sketch: Given a declared reviewer POV, ProjectFromPOV[AllNodes[], "reviewer"] should be preflight-checkable against a profile capability and equivalent to the explicit filter bundle for the fixture graph.
